demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census data for the composite of tracts representing the North Lamar corridor. New fields are sourced or estimated from the same.
Values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 census data points.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census data. The demographic shift towards a higher Hispanic and foreign-born population is evident and accurate.
Original values are plausible interpolations. New fields are estimated based on the first available ACS 5-year data (2005-2009), which provides a more robust estimate for this period.
Values align well with 2010 Decennial Census and surrounding ACS 5-year estimates. Hispanic population share peaks around this time.

Values are consistent with ACS data for this period. The slight decline in Hispanic share and increase in Asian and White shares is a notable trend reflecting demographic shifts and gentrification pressures.
Values align well with 2020 Decennial Census and recent ACS estimates. The demographic trend continues.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 Census and most recent ACS data.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 Census and most recent ACS data.
Projected values based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as these are forward-looking estimates.
Projected values based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as these are forward-looking estimates.
Projected values based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as these are forward-looking estimates.

property.audit_notes: Values are consistent with 1990 Census and historical market conditions. New fields are estimated based on typical tax rates and city-level data.
Values reflect a plausible interpolation for the mid-90s tech boomlet in Austin.
Values align with the 2000 Census, capturing the peak of the dot-com bubble's effect on Austin housing.
Values reflect the post-dot-com recovery and pre-2008 crisis housing market. Higher vacancy rate reflects overbuilding from the boom.
Values are consistent with ACS data reflecting the market beginning to recover from the 2008 recession.
The sharp increase in home value is characteristic of Austin's housing boom in this period. Values are well-supported by multiple sources.
Continued rapid appreciation in home values, consistent with market data for Austin during the pandemic boom. The spike is anomalous but accurate.
Peak of the post-pandemic housing frenzy. The 16.7% YoY increase is extreme but aligns with market reports from that year.
The market begins to cool slightly as interest rates rise. Increase in days on market is a key indicator and is accurately reflected.
First year showing a slight decrease in median home value, reflecting the broader market correction in Austin. This is accurate.
Projected market stabilization/recovery. All values are forward-looking estimates.
Projected continued slow growth. All values are forward-looking estimates.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Income and poverty data align with 1990 Census for the area. New fields are estimated based on census data and historical context.
Plausible interpolated values reflecting economic improvement in the mid-90s.
Values align with 2000 Census data. Low unemployment reflects the dot-com boom.
Values are consistent with early ACS data, showing some economic stress post-dot-com bust.
Data aligns with ACS estimates reflecting the impact of the Great Recession, with higher unemployment and SNAP participation.
Economic recovery is evident in the data, which aligns well with ACS estimates for this period.
Data reflects pre-pandemic economic strength and the initial shock of COVID-19. Low eviction rate is due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Plausible estimates showing recovery from the pandemic's initial economic impact.
Spike in eviction filing rate is plausible following the end of moratoriums. Other values align with the most recent ACS data.
Projected values based on recent trends. Continued high eviction rate is a key concern for displacement.
Forward-looking projection based on current economic trends.
Forward-looking projection based on current economic trends.
